steps.title: Identify the gap in Wade's evidence
steps.content: Wade shows the three pieces display features of live performance—addressing audiences, simple rhyming for easy memorization, requests for payment—but these formal features alone don't prove they originate from a minstrel working in Heege's own region.

steps.title: Find what proves the regional origin
steps.content: A directly establishes the regional tie: references to local villages show the pieces were designed for audiences in that specific area, proving they weren't just any performance pieces but specifically ones crafted for local performance—supporting Wade's claim they came from a nearby minstrel's repertoire. B, C, and D fail to establish this crucial regional connection. That matches choice A — A is correct.
